facts.statement: Alphabet reports Google Services as a segment that includes ads, Android, Chrome, devices, Google Maps, Google Play, Search, and YouTube; the segment generates revenues primarily from advertising, consumer subscription-based products such as YouTube TV, YouTube Music and Premium, NFL Sunday Ticket and Google One, app and in-app purchase sales, and devices.

Alphabet reports Google Cloud as a segment that includes infrastructure and platform services, applications, and other services for enterprise customers; Google Cloud generates revenues primarily from consumption-based fees and subscriptions for Google Cloud Platform services, Google Workspace communication and collaboration tools, and other enterprise services.

Alphabet reports Other Bets as a combination of multiple operating segments that are not individually material, with revenues generated primarily from the sale of autonomous transportation services and internet services.
Alphabet reported total revenues of $402.836 billion for the year ended December 31, 2025, including a $127 million hedging loss line item in the segment revenue table.
Google Services revenue was $342.721 billion in 2025, up from $304.930 billion in 2024 and $272.543 billion in 2023 as presented in Alphabet's segment table.
Google Cloud revenue was $58.705 billion in 2025, up from $43.229 billion in 2024 and $33.088 billion in 2023 as presented in Alphabet's segment table.
Other Bets revenue was $1.537 billion in 2025, compared with $1.648 billion in 2024 and $1.527 billion in 2023.
Alphabet reported total income from operations of $129.039 billion for 2025, compared with $112.390 billion in 2024 and $84.293 billion in 2023.
Google Services operating income was $139.404 billion in 2025, compared with $121.263 billion in 2024 and $95.858 billion in 2023.
Google Cloud operating income was $13.910 billion in 2025, compared with $6.112 billion in 2024 and $1.716 billion in 2023.
Other Bets reported an operating loss of $7.515 billion in 2025, compared with operating losses of $4.444 billion in 2024 and $4.095 billion in 2023.

Alphabet-level activities were a $16.760 billion reduction to 2025 operating income; the company says these costs primarily include certain AI-focused shared research and development activities, corporate initiatives such as philanthropic activities, and corporate shared costs such as finance, human resources, and legal costs including certain fines and settlements.
The SEC company facts summary reports Alphabet research and development expense of $45.427 billion for FY2025, filed on 2026-02-05.
The SEC company facts summary reports Alphabet operating cash flow of $101.746 billion for FY2025, filed on 2026-02-05.

The SEC company facts summary reports Alphabet stockholders' equity of $256.144 billion, long-term debt of $10.883 billion, and cash and equivalents of $23.466 billion for FY2025, filed on 2026-02-05.
Alphabet reported long-lived assets of $261.818 billion as of December 31, 2025, consisting of $195.337 billion in the United States and $66.481 billion internationally.

Alphabet identifies Chief Executive Officer Sundar Pichai as the Chief Operating Decision Maker, and states that he uses segment operating income or loss to allocate resources in the annual planning process and assess segment performance primarily by monitoring actual results versus the annual plan.

The 2025 Form 10-K was signed by Sundar Pichai as Chief Executive Officer and Director, Anat Ashkenazi as Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, and Amie Thuener O'Toole as Vice President, Corporate Controller and Principal Accounting Officer.

Alphabet's 2026 proxy lists 10 director nominees and states that 70% are independent; the nominees are Larry Page, Sergey Brin, Sundar Pichai, John L. Hennessy, Frances H. Arnold, R. Martin "Marty" Chávez, L. John Doerr, Roger W. Ferguson Jr., K. Ram Shriram, and Robin L. Washington.

Alphabet's 2026 proxy states that the Board has an independent Chair separate from the CEO role and that the Audit Committee, Risk and Compliance Committee, Leadership Development, Inclusion and Compensation Committee, and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee are 100% independent.

Alphabet established a Risk and Compliance Committee in October 2025 to provide specialized focus on the evolving landscape of regulatory and operational risks, according to the Chair of the Board's letter in the 2026 proxy.

Alphabet's 2026 annual meeting agenda includes a board-recommended proposal to approve an amendment and restatement of the Amended and Restated 2021 Stock Plan to increase the share reserve by 200,000,000 shares of Class C capital stock.

Alphabet's 2021 Stock Plan includes a recoupment/clawback provision stating that Alphabet is entitled, to the extent permitted or required by law, Alphabet policy, or exchange requirements, to recoup compensation paid under the plan.

Alphabet reported $11.512 billion of gross unrecognized tax benefits as of December 31, 2025; it also disclosed that the IRS is examining its 2019 through 2021 tax returns and that it has received tax assessments in multiple foreign jurisdictions asserting transfer pricing adjustments or permanent establishment.
